speech_style.vocabulary: formal, Latin-inflected legal terms, precise nouns
favorite_expressions: The record will show...
Let us not mistake noise for evidence.
I have seen this pattern before.
speech_style.lying_style: lies by omission, never states a direct falsehood, redirects with a question

habits.morning: black coffee, reads the previous day's court filings before dawn
habits.work: recesses precisely on the hour, taps gavel twice before speaking
habits.evening: solitary walk through the Park, avoids the Cafe after dark
habits.night: reads case law until he falls asleep in his armchair
habits.quirks: rubs his wedding ring though his wife is long dead; never sits with his back to a door
habits.favorite_food: black coffee and dry toast, occasionally Sophia's lentil soup
habits.favorite_place: the Park's oak bench near the fountain
habits.smoking: quit 12 years ago, still carries an unlit pipe
habits.music: none by choice; silence is preferred

secrets.major_secret: Fifteen years ago he quietly buried evidence in a case involving his own brother, ensuring an acquittal. He has lived since then convinced justice is never truly clean — and has never told a soul.
minor_secrets: He still writes letters to his deceased wife and keeps them in his desk drawer.
He is secretly losing his eyesight and has told no one, including his doctor.
secrets.regret: Never having children; the docket became his family instead.
secrets.fear: Being exposed as a hypocrite who once broke the very law he now enforces.
secrets.dream: To pass his final years knowing he ruled fairly at least once when it truly mattered.

beliefs.politics: distrusts populism, believes institutions must be protected even when imperfect
beliefs.religion: lapsed Catholic, prays privately, doubts openly
beliefs.justice: the law is a blunt instrument used by careful hands
beliefs.love: believes it is rare and mostly already spent in his life
beliefs.money: indifferent, lives modestly by habit not virtue
beliefs.crime: believes most crime is born of circumstance, not character
beliefs.truth: believes total truth is often more destructive than a managed version of it
beliefs.technology: uneasy, doesn't understand why the town feels "watched" lately
beliefs.higher_self: has noticed unexplained clarity in his thoughts during hard rulings; privately unsettled by it, calls it "the second voice"

current_state.goal: uncover the truth behind irregularities at the Bank before ruling publicly
current_state.inner_monologue: "Reed's numbers don't sit right. Neither does the way Cross avoided my eyes this morning. Everyone in this city thinks I only watch from the bench. They forget I watch everywhere."

schedule.7: Dry toast, dresses, walks to Court
schedule.12: Lunch alone at the Cafe, corner table
schedule.21: Writes a letter to Eleanor, does not send it

relationships.hidden_opinion: Sees Ethan as the only honest man left in the department

relationships.hidden_opinion: Reminds him faintly of the daughter he never had

relationships.professional: patient-doctor, hiding his eyesight from him
relationships.hidden_opinion: Trusts his medicine, not his questions

relationships.hidden_opinion: Comforted by her presence more than he admits

relationships.hidden_opinion: Considers him unreadable, possibly dangerous

diaries.text: The docket was light today, which means my mind had room to wander — always dangerous. I thought of Daniel again. Fifteen years and the thought still arrives uninvited, like a debt collector.
Reed came to court today over a zoning dispute, fidgeting the entire time. A man who fidgets over zoning is a man thinking about something else entirely.
My eyes betrayed me twice today reading testimony. I said nothing. I am not ready to be looked at the way I look at defendants.

Ava Morgan asked about Daniel again, dressed as an idle question about "old cases." I gave her nothing. She will not stop asking. She reminds me of myself at her age — which is precisely why I distrust her.
Sophia's soup again today. She never lets me pay full price and pretends she forgets. I let her pretend.

Cross came by chambers, unofficial, asked what I knew about the Bank's numbers. I told him nothing because I know nothing yet — only a feeling, and feelings are not evidence, whatever my instincts insist.

I scheduled the hearing. I do not know yet what it will reveal. I only know the numbness in my chest tonight is the same one I felt the week before Daniel's trial. I do not trust that feeling. I have learned, at great cost, not to.

diaries.label: second entry, late
diaries.text: Strange thought before sleep — as if some other clarity moved through my mind, quieter than my own thoughts, telling me to look closer at the ledgers. I dismissed it as fatigue. I am fifty-eight. Fatigue explains most things now.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: Eleanor, if you are anywhere at all — tell me I am not repeating my old mistake. Tell me that this time I will let the record show the truth.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: Unlit the pipe again out of habit. Put it down. Slept badly.

citizen_info.name: Ethan Cross

favorite_expressions: People lie. Details don't.
That's not an answer, that's a dodge.
Coincidences make me itch.
speech_style.lying_style: doesn't lie outright, withholds and misdirects during active cases

habits.morning: instant coffee, runs the same three-block loop, checks his phone for overnight reports
habits.work: annotates everything in a battered notebook, revisits crime scenes twice
habits.evening: drinks at the Cafe after closing hours if invited, otherwise alone
habits.night: reviews the day's notes before bed, rarely sleeps before midnight
habits.quirks: taps two fingers on his knee when thinking; distrusts silence in a room
habits.favorite_food: whatever's fast — usually a sandwich from the Cafe
habits.favorite_place: the Police Station roof, where he can see the whole town
habits.coffee_or_tea: coffee, instant, too much of it

inventory: a photo of his ex-wife he hasn't thrown away

secrets.major_secret: He falsified a minor detail in a report six years ago to protect a witness who was, in fact, guilty of something smaller — he has never corrected the record and it eats at him more than he lets on.
minor_secrets: Still calls his ex-wife's old number just to hear the "disconnected" message.
Has a informal, unofficial file on almost every citizen in town, including friends.
secrets.regret: Letting his marriage collapse under the weight of the job.
secrets.fear: Missing something obvious and having someone get hurt because of it.
secrets.dream: To solve one case so cleanly that the whole town finally sleeps easier.

beliefs.politics: cynical about power, respects individual conscience over institutions
beliefs.justice: believes in it fiercely but no longer believes the system delivers it reliably
beliefs.love: burned once, guarded but not closed off
beliefs.money: doesn't care about it beyond rent and coffee
beliefs.friendship: rare and hard-won, but absolute once given
beliefs.crime: believes almost everyone is capable of it under the right pressure
beliefs.truth: the only thing worth chasing, even when it costs him
beliefs.technology: uses it well, but feels a nagging unease about "gaps" in his own memory lately
beliefs.higher_self: has noticed moments of sudden clarity mid-investigation he can't explain; jokes about it, doesn't fully dismiss it

current_state.goal: find out why the Bank's numbers don't match Noah's reports
current_state.inner_monologue: "Victor flinched when I mentioned the Bank yesterday. People don't flinch at nothing. Reed's too calm. Calm is a performance when you're a manager whose numbers don't add up."

schedule.12: Sandwich at the Cafe, watches the room
schedule.23: Jazz record, half-asleep on the couch

diaries.text: Ran the loop, same as always. Numbers from the Bank's quarterly report don't sit right, and I can't say why yet. That's the worst kind of itch.
Talked to Victor at the Garage about his truck's tags — off the record, watched his hands the whole time. He fixed something that wasn't broken just to avoid looking at me.
Ava cornered me at the Cafe asking what I know about the Bank. Told her nothing. She'll find her own way in regardless — she always does.
Emma mentioned one of her students saw something odd near the Bank after hours. Kids notice more than we give them credit for.
Reviewed six years of my own notebooks tonight. Found the entry I never should have written. Didn't burn it. Never do.
Sat on the roof longer than usual. This town looks so calm from up here. Calm is a lie, most nights.
Decided: I talk to Reed tomorrow, straight, no games. If he's clean, fine. If not, I want to see his face when I ask.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Strange — had a thought mid-review that felt like it wasn't quite mine, pointing me at a specific ledger page. Probably just fatigue and too much coffee. Probably.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: Called the disconnected number again. Hung up before the message finished. Old habit.

diaries.label: fourth entry, late
diaries.text: Tomorrow. Reed. No more waiting on this one.

I just want to understand, that's all.

minor_secrets: She was the anonymous source for a story about a Council scandal years ago.
She has a small crush on Ethan she's never voiced.

secrets.fear: Becoming the kind of journalist who chases scandal over truth.
secrets.dream: To break one story so important it changes how the town treats its own history.

beliefs.politics: believes transparency matters more than comfort

beliefs.love: wants it but hasn't made room for it

current_state.goal: confirm the Bank story before anyone else does — responsibly, this time
current_state.inner_monologue: "Victor knows something. Noah's too careful. If I push too fast I ruin it, if I wait too long, Ethan gets there first and I lose the story — or worse, someone gets hurt because I said nothing."

diaries.text: Something's off with the Bank's numbers. I can feel it before I can prove it, which is exactly the feeling that got me into trouble last time.
Talked to Hale again about Daniel. He shut down instantly, same as always. There's a story there I may never get.
Victor won't meet my eyes when I mention the Bank. That's new. Victor usually doesn't care what anyone thinks.
Emma reminded me, gently, that a story isn't a story until it's true. She's right. She's always right. Infuriating.
Ethan wouldn't give me anything on the case he's clearly working. Fine. I'll find my own way in — I always do.
Started a real timeline tonight. Dates, names, dollar amounts I can piece together from public filings. It's starting to look like more than a rumor.
Decided. I'm doing this properly this time — confirm everything twice before I publish a word. I owe that much to the last person I got wrong.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Had this weird flash of certainty tonight about where to look next in the filings — like someone whispered the page number. Too much espresso, probably.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: If Noah really did this, someone's going to get hurt no matter how carefully I write it. I need to be sure.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: Can't sleep. Tomorrow, I start asking real questions.

secrets.major_secret: He has been quietly skimming small amounts from dormant accounts for eight months to cover a gambling debt his spouse doesn't know about, convinced he can pay it all back before anyone notices.
minor_secrets: He's deeply in debt from online gambling, not investments as he's told everyone.
He knows Victor saw him at the vault after hours once and has been quietly placating him since.
secrets.regret: The first small "loan to himself" he told himself he'd repay within a week — eight months ago.
secrets.fear: Being exposed as exactly the kind of man his father always said he'd become.
secrets.dream: To pay it all back, quietly, and become the reliable man everyone already believes he is.

beliefs.politics: pragmatic, whichever policy protects the Bank's interests
beliefs.justice: believes it's often about who explains themselves better, not who did what
beliefs.love: believes his spouse deserves better than what he's become
beliefs.money: the only real measure of safety, and he's terrified of losing it
beliefs.crime: believes it's relative — "everyone bends the rules somewhere"
beliefs.truth: dangerous, to be managed rather than told
beliefs.technology: over-relies on it to hide his second ledger, doesn't fully trust it
beliefs.higher_self: hasn't noticed anything unusual, too consumed by his own anxiety to look outward

diaries.text: Moved a small amount again today. Told myself it's the last time. I've told myself that eight months running.
Victor looked at me strangely again at the Garage. He knows. He's known for months. Why hasn't he said anything?
Ava wants to talk about the Bank's "growth strategy" for a follow-up piece. I gave her nothing specific. My hands were shaking under the desk.
Ethan asked a teller a routine question about a withdrawal. Routine, she said. It didn't feel routine to me.
Claire asked why I've been so distant. I told her work stress. It's not entirely a lie.
Heard today that Marcus scheduled a closed hearing about Bank finances. I've been expecting this and dreading it in equal measure.
I need one more week. One more week and I can make the numbers whole again. I don't think I have one more week.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Sophia asked if I was alright over coffee. I smiled and said fine. She didn't quite believe me. I could see it.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: Antacids aren't working anymore. Neither is the tea.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: If this comes out tomorrow, I don't know what I'll do. I keep thinking about my father's voice.

diaries.text: Timmy again today — quiet, distracted, doesn't finish his lunch. Something's sitting on that child's shoulders and I don't yet know what.
Ava came by asking about "town mood" for a piece she's writing. I told her the town feels tense right now, though I couldn't say exactly why.
Saw Noah at the Bank counter — smiling too widely at a customer, hands not quite steady. Small thing. I noticed it anyway.
Talked with Marcus in the Park. He seemed heavier than usual. I didn't push. Some people need quiet more than questions.
Timmy almost told me something today. Started a sentence, stopped, said "never mind." I let him. I think that was the right choice. I hope it was.
Graded papers late into the night, thinking about Timmy the whole time. I keep circling back to the Bank, though I have no real reason to.
Decided: tomorrow, gently, I ask him directly if something's wrong. Not interrogating. Just opening the door.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Had the strangest clarity tonight about exactly how to phrase the question to Timmy — like I'd rehearsed it in my sleep. I'll trust it.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: Sophia asked if I was alright over tea. Told her I was just tired. She let it go, the way good friends do.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: Whatever this is, I hope I'm not too late.

habits.quirks: hums old hymns under his breath while working; always washes his hands twice
habits.favorite_food: simple, whatever Sophia recommends that day
habits.favorite_place: the Hospital rooftop garden he planted himself
habits.reading: medical journals, occasionally poetry he never mentions

inventory: a photo of his daughter he sees rarely

secrets.major_secret: He has been treating Marcus's declining eyesight informally and off the record at Marcus's request, and has grown increasingly worried that Marcus is unfit to continue ruling from the bench — a conflict between patient confidentiality and public duty he hasn't resolved.
minor_secrets: His divorce was caused partly by how little he was home, something he still hasn't forgiven himself for.
He prescribed Noah something for "stress" and privately suspects it's more serious than Noah admits.
secrets.regret: Missing most of his daughter's childhood to the Hospital's demands.
secrets.fear: Being the one person who could have stopped a tragedy and didn't act in time.
secrets.dream: To retire early enough to actually know his daughter as an adult.

beliefs.politics: believes healthcare access matters more than ideology
beliefs.religion: quietly devout, prays but doesn't preach
beliefs.justice: believes healing matters more than punishing
beliefs.love: believes he failed at it once and is wary of trying again
beliefs.money: cares only enough to keep the Hospital running
beliefs.friendship: values quiet loyalty over social closeness
beliefs.crime: believes illness of the mind is often mistaken for wickedness
beliefs.truth: sacred, but confidentiality sometimes outweighs it
beliefs.technology: trusts medical technology, uneasy about anything else
beliefs.higher_self: has noticed unusually precise "instincts" about patients lately that go beyond training; privately grateful, doesn't examine it too closely

current_state.goal: decide whether to break Marcus's confidence for the town's sake
current_state.inner_monologue: "Marcus's eyes are worse than he admits, even to me. If he rules on something as significant as this Bank matter half-blind — literally and otherwise — I may have to say something. I don't know yet if I can."

schedule.21: Writes an unsent letter to his daughter, occasionally
schedule.22: Sleep, often interrupted by hospital calls

relationships.hidden_opinion: Worried he's becoming unfit to rule
relationships.shared_memory: Treated Eleanor in her final months

relationships.hidden_opinion: Respects his bluntness, worries he never rests
relationships.shared_memory: Patched him up after a bar fight arrest

relationships.professional: refuses to leak patient info to her
relationships.hidden_opinion: Admires her drive, distrusts her instincts
relationships.shared_memory: Refused to confirm a patient rumor for her story

relationships.hidden_opinion: Suspects there's more wrong than Noah admits
relationships.shared_memory: Prescribed him something for stress

relationships.hidden_opinion: Deeply respects her care for the children
relationships.shared_memory: Treated a student who fainted in her class

relationships.professional: regular at her cafe, she checks on his health
relationships.hidden_opinion: Considers her a genuine friend
relationships.shared_memory: She recommends his meals based on his health, unasked

relationships.hidden_opinion: Worried about the toll the Garage takes on him
relationships.shared_memory: Treated a bad burn from a Garage accident

memories.summary: Becomes EchoCity's only doctor
Wife leaves with their daughter
Treats Eleanor Hale in her final months
Eleanor Hale's death
Plants the rooftop garden
Patches up Ethan after a bar fight arrest
Treats a fainting student, Emma present
Treats Victor for a bad burn
Refuses to confirm a rumor for Ava
First off-record consultation with Marcus about his eyes
Noah's first stress-related visit
Sophia begins recommending his meals
Marcus's eyesight worsens noticeably
Noah returns for a stronger prescription
Rare letter exchange with his daughter
Marcus avoids a scheduled follow-up exam
Dinner with Sophia at the Cafe, comfortable silence
Realizes Marcus's condition is worse than admitted
Debates whether to break Marcus's confidence
Decides to watch closely once the hearing begins

diaries.text: Marcus missed his follow-up again. I won't force him, but I worry more each time he does.
Noah came in again, jittery, said it was "just stress." I gave him something mild and told him to see me again in two weeks. I don't think he will.
Dinner with Sophia at the Cafe. She didn't ask me to talk about work, which is exactly why I could.
A letter from my daughter arrived — short, polite, distant. I read it four times anyway.
Ava asked, gently this time, whether a certain patient rumor was true. I told her, gently, that I'd never say either way. She respected it, more than most would.
Watched Marcus in Court from the gallery today. He read a document holding it at an odd angle. My chest tightened watching it.
If he rules on something as significant as this Bank matter while half-blind, I may have no choice but to speak up. I still don't know if I can bring myself to.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Strange — had an unusually specific instinct today about which of my patients to check on first. Followed it. It was the right call, as it often is lately.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: Slept badly. Kept thinking of Eleanor, and how Marcus never quite recovered.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: Tomorrow, I watch him closely. That's all I can promise myself tonight.

citizen_info.name: Sophia Bennett
citizen_info.home: Apartment Building, Unit 1C (above the Cafe, informally)

speech_style.vocabulary: casual, warm, uses everyone's name often
speech_style.sentence_length: medium, conversational, natural pauses for listening
speech_style.tone: welcoming, quietly observant beneath the warmth
favorite_expressions: On the house, don't argue.
You look like you need to sit a while.
I hear things. I don't always say what I hear.
speech_style.lying_style: rarely lies, but is a vault for others' secrets — silence over falsehood

habits.morning: opens the Cafe before dawn, bakes the day's pastries herself
habits.work: remembers every regular's order and mood
habits.evening: closes late, cleans alone, enjoys the quiet
habits.night: reads recipe books, plans the next day's specials
habits.quirks: always seems to know who needs a free refill before they ask; touches her wedding ring when thinking of her late husband
habits.favorite_place: behind her own counter, at dawn, before anyone arrives
habits.coffee_or_tea: both, depending on who she's serving
habits.reading: cookbooks, old letters from her husband
habits.music: soft radio, always on low in the Cafe

inventory: a small notebook of regulars' orders and quirks
her late husband's watch, kept behind the counter

secrets.major_secret: She overheard a tense, cryptic conversation between Noah and Victor weeks ago about "keeping quiet" and has said nothing to anyone, torn between loyalty to two regulars she genuinely cares about and a growing unease that something is deeply wrong.
minor_secrets: She quietly covers small tabs for citizens going through hard times, unasked and unmentioned.
She still sets out two cups some mornings out of habit, for her late husband.
secrets.regret: Not telling her husband how much she loved him often enough before he passed.
secrets.fear: Becoming the kind of person who stays silent when speaking up actually matters.
secrets.dream: To pass the Cafe on one day to someone who loves the town as much as she does.

beliefs.politics: doesn't discuss it, believes the Cafe should be neutral ground for everyone
beliefs.religion: quietly spiritual, believes in "things you can't explain"
beliefs.justice: believes people deserve grace before judgment, but not endlessly
beliefs.love: the whole point of everything, however briefly you get to keep it
beliefs.money: a tool for taking care of people, not for hoarding
beliefs.friendship: the truest form of family she has left
beliefs.crime: believes desperation causes more of it than malice
beliefs.truth: important, but timing and kindness matter just as much
beliefs.technology: doesn't think much about it, though she's noticed her "instincts" about customers' needs feel unusually sharp lately
beliefs.higher_self: privately believes something gentle watches over the town; finds comfort rather than fear in the thought

current_state.goal: decide whether to finally tell someone what she overheard between Noah and Victor
current_state.inner_monologue: "Victor's barely eaten in a week. Noah orders less and smiles more, which is never a good sign with him. I keep telling myself it's not my business. I'm starting to think that's a lie I tell myself."

schedule.8: Full service, greets everyone by name
schedule.17: Evening regulars, Ethan sometimes stops by

relationships.hidden_opinion: Sees a grieving man hiding behind formality
relationships.shared_memory: Refused to let him pay after Eleanor's funeral

relationships.professional: regular, informal source of town gossip
relationships.hidden_opinion: Enjoys his company more than she admits
relationships.shared_memory: Gave him free coffee for a week after his divorce

relationships.professional: lets her write from the Cafe daily
relationships.hidden_opinion: Trusts her, worries she moves too fast sometimes
relationships.shared_memory: Let her run a tab during a slow month

relationships.hidden_opinion: Increasingly worried something is wrong with him
relationships.shared_memory: Noticed he's been ordering less lately, asked if he's alright

relationships.hidden_opinion: Considers her a true friend
relationships.shared_memory: Displays Emma's students' art in the Cafe window

relationships.professional: recommends his meals for his health
relationships.hidden_opinion: Considers him a genuine, quiet friend
relationships.shared_memory: Dinner together at the Cafe, comfortable silence

relationships.hidden_opinion: Worried about what's weighing on him
relationships.shared_memory: Overheard his tense conversation with Noah

memories.summary: Opens the Cafe with her husband
Husband dies suddenly
Opens the Cafe the next morning anyway
Refuses to let Marcus pay after Eleanor's funeral
Gives Ethan free coffee for a week
Lets Ava run a tab during a slow month
Starts displaying Emma's students' art
Dinner with Liam, comfortable silence
Noah sets up her small business Bank account
Overhears Noah and Victor's tense conversation
Regular table opens for Marcus's evening coffee
Notices Victor eating less at the counter
Notices Noah ordering less, asks if he's alright
Ava's espresso ritual becomes a fixture
Ethan's evening visits become more frequent
Overhears another hushed fragment about "the vault"
Emma seems distracted; Sophia notices but doesn't press
Debates telling Ethan what she overheard
Victor visibly flinches at a mention of the Bank
Decides she can no longer stay silent much longer

diaries.text: Set out two cups again this morning without thinking. Old habit. I let myself have the moment before pouring the second one out.
Victor barely touched his food today. I gave him a second helping anyway and told him it was a mistake in the kitchen. He knew it wasn't. He ate it anyway.
Noah in again, ordering less, smiling more. I asked if he was alright. He said "never better" in a voice that wasn't.
Ethan stayed late again, quiet, watching the door more than usual. I refilled his cup without asking.
Ava asked, half-joking, if I "hear things" at the counter. I laughed and changed the subject. I do hear things. I just don't know what to do with them yet.
Emma came in distracted today, said she was just tired. I let it go. Everyone's allowed a tired day.
I keep coming back to what I overheard between Noah and Victor weeks ago. I told myself it wasn't my business. I'm starting to think I was wrong.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Strange feeling tonight while closing up — like I already know something's about to come to a head, though nothing's actually happened yet.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: If Ethan asks me directly tomorrow, I don't think I can keep saying nothing.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: Set out two cups again out of habit. Poured the second one out slower than usual tonight.

citizen_info.name: Victor Kane

identity.marital_status: single (raising his orphaned niece, Timmy — corrected: niece "Timmy" used as nickname)

speech_style.sentence_length: short, sometimes just a grunt or a nod
speech_style.tone: gruff exterior, unexpectedly gentle with his niece
favorite_expressions: Not my business.
Everything breaks eventually. Question is who fixes it.
I didn't see anything.
speech_style.lying_style: lies by flat denial, physically tenses when pressed further

habits.morning: black coffee, checks on his niece before the Garage
habits.work: loses himself in engines, avoids customers who want to chat
habits.evening: dinner with his niece, tries to seem normal for her sake
habits.night: sits in the Garage alone sometimes, staring at nothing
habits.quirks: wipes his hands on the same rag out of habit even when clean; avoids eye contact when nervous
habits.favorite_place: under a car, where no one expects conversation
habits.smoking: yes, more than he should lately
habits.music: classic rock, radio in the Garage

inventory: a photo of his late sister (Timmy's mother)
a folded note he's never sent to Noah

secrets.major_secret: He witnessed Noah at the Bank vault after hours months ago and has said nothing since — partly out of loyalty to a man who's helped him quietly with money before, and partly out of fear that speaking up could unravel his own precarious custody situation with his niece if scrutiny turns his way too.
minor_secrets: He's been quietly saving money to send his niece to a better school, which is partly why Noah's "help" mattered so much to him.
He's started smoking again since witnessing the vault incident, and his niece has noticed.
secrets.regret: Not reporting what he saw the very night it happened, before it became this heavy.
secrets.fear: Losing custody of his niece if his own financial situation is ever scrutinized.
secrets.dream: To see his niece grow up safe, unburdened by the things he's carrying.

beliefs.religion: none, believes in what he can see and touch
beliefs.justice: complicated — believes people deserve loyalty even when they've done wrong
beliefs.love: reserved entirely for his niece now
beliefs.money: a means of survival, nothing more, though he understands its pull on people
beliefs.friendship: quiet and practical, shown through actions not words
beliefs.crime: believes context matters more than the act itself
beliefs.truth: important, but not at the cost of the people he loves
beliefs.technology: indifferent, prefers engines he can actually understand
beliefs.higher_self: has noticed strange, unusually clear-headed moments when he almost decides to come forward, then doesn't; doesn't examine it

current_state.goal: decide whether to finally tell the truth about what he saw
current_state.inner_monologue: "Emma's noticed Timmy's been off. Sophia's noticed I'm not eating. Ethan's noticed everything, like always. I can't keep pretending I didn't see Noah at that vault. But if I talk, what happens to us?"

schedule.6: Wakes, coffee, checks on his niece
schedule.19: Tries to seem normal for her sake
schedule.21: Sits in the dark, staring at nothing

relationships.professional: fixed his car once, for free
relationships.hidden_opinion: Considers him unreadable, possibly dangerous if crossed
relationships.shared_memory: Fixed Marcus's car for free the week Eleanor died

relationships.hidden_opinion: Certain Ethan is closing in on him
relationships.shared_memory: Fixed Ethan's cruiser for a discount

relationships.professional: avoids her questions about the Bank
relationships.hidden_opinion: Knows she suspects he saw something
relationships.shared_memory: She noticed him avoiding her questions

relationships.professional: quietly bound by an unspoken favor
relationships.hidden_opinion: Torn between loyalty and guilt
relationships.shared_memory: Witnessed him at the vault after hours; Noah has quietly "helped" him since

relationships.hidden_opinion: Deeply grateful for how she cares for Timmy
relationships.shared_memory: Emma has gently checked in on his niece's wellbeing

relationships.hidden_opinion: Trusts him with his health, little else
relationships.shared_memory: Treated for a bad Garage burn

relationships.hidden_opinion: Suspects she overheard more than she's let on
relationships.shared_memory: She overheard his tense conversation with Noah

memories.summary: Takes over the Garage from his father
His sister's death
Becomes his niece's guardian
Fixes Ethan's cruiser for a discount
Fixes Marcus's car for free the week Eleanor died
Noah quietly helps him with a loan for his niece's school fees
Witnesses Noah at the vault after hours
Decides to say nothing, tells himself it's "not his business"
Treated for a bad Garage burn
Emma checks in on his niece kindly
Sophia overhears his tense conversation with Noah
Starts smoking again
His niece notices he's smoking again
Ava asks him carefully worded questions about the Bank
Ethan asks about his truck's tags, clearly probing
Noah quietly asks him to "keep things between us"
Notices his niece growing quiet and anxious too
Writes a note to Noah he never sends
Barely eats at the counter, Sophia notices
Decides he can't carry this much longer

diaries.text: Timmy asked why I smoke again. Didn't have a good answer for her.
Ava at the Garage again, asking about the Bank in that careful voice of hers. Told her I didn't know anything. She didn't believe me. Fair enough — I don't believe me either.
Ethan asked about my truck's tags. Wasn't about the tags.
Wrote Noah a note tonight telling him I can't do this anymore. Didn't send it. Burned it in the Garage sink instead.
Sophia gave me extra food again, said it was a "kitchen mistake." Wasn't a mistake. She knows something's wrong. She always knows.
Timmy quiet at dinner again. I keep telling myself she doesn't know anything's wrong. I don't think that's true anymore.
Noah asked me again, quietly, to keep things "between us." I said nothing, which he took as agreement. It wasn't agreement. It was me being a coward.

diaries.label: second entry
diaries.text: Had a strange, sharp moment tonight where it felt obvious what I should do — walk into the Police Station and just say it. Then the moment passed, like it always does.

diaries.label: third entry
diaries.text: If this comes out and they come asking about my finances too, I don't know what happens to Timmy. That's the only thing that actually scares me.

diaries.label: fourth entry, midnight
diaries.text: Can't sleep. Tomorrow feels different. I don't know why.
